canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, CB is consistently characterized as a benchmark for evaluating natural language inference (NLI). The most prevalent definition describes it as a "textual entailment benchmark" for "short premise-hypothesis pairs," and it is frequently identified as a component of the SuperGLUE benchmark. Papers use CB to measure the behavior of `Textual entailment`. A more specific framing, present in several sources, defines the task as evaluating whether a statement "commits the author to a particular belief" or a certain entailment, with outputs classified as entailment, contradiction, or neutrality. While the focus is almost always on NLI, one paper presents a broader view, categorizing CB as a "general NLU task" alongside TREC and Subj. In evaluations, CB is often grouped with other NLI and SuperGLUE tasks, such as RTE, WiC, BoolQ, and COPA, with one source noting it is "very similar to RTE".
papers: Innovative Image Fraud Detection with Cross-Sample Anomaly Analysis: The Power ofLLMs
The time scale of redundancy between prosody and linguistic context
CARMA: Enhanced Compositionality inLLMs via Advanced Regularisation and Mutual Information Alignment
Batch Calibration: Rethinking Calibration for In-Context Learning and Prompt Engineering
Grounding Multimodal Large Language Models to the World
PromptAgent: Strategic Planning with Language Models Enables Expert-level Prompt Optimization
Two-stage LLM Fine-tuning with Less Specialization and More Generalization
Addax: Utilizing Zeroth-Order Gradients to Improve Memory Efficiency and Performance of SGD for Fine-Tuning Language Models
Enhancing Zeroth-order Fine-tuning for Language Models with Low-rank Structures
Mini-batch Coresets for Memory-efficient Language Model Training on Data Mixtures
Second-Order Fine-Tuning without Pain for LLMs: A Hessian Informed Zeroth-Order Optimizer
Zeroth-Order Fine-Tuning of LLMs with Transferable Static Sparsity

definitions.text: A textual entailment benchmark used to evaluate natural language inference on short premise-hypothesis pairs.
definitions.paper: Batch Calibration: Rethinking Calibration for In-Context Learning and Prompt Engineering

relationships.paper: Enhancing In-Context Learning Performance with just SVD-Based Weight Pruning: A Theoretical Perspective
